sn20.10:1.2: And on that occasion a certain newly-ordained monk was spending too much time visiting lay families.
sn20.10:1.4: “Friend, don’t spend so much time visiting lay families.”
sn20.10:1.5: He, admonished by the monks, didn’t desist.
sn20.10:1.6: Then many monks went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side. As they were sitting there, they said to him,
sn20.10:1.7: “Lord, there is a certain newly-ordained monk who is spending too much time visiting lay families.
sn20.10:1.8: The monks have said to him,
sn20.10:1.9: ‘Friend, don’t spend so much time visiting lay families.’
sn20.10:1.10: He, admonished by the monks, hasn’t desisted.”
sn20.10:2.1: [The Blessed One said:] “Once, monks, a cat was standing by an alley-drain-rubbish bin to ambush a mouse, (thinking,)
sn20.10:2.2: ‘When a mouse comes out to forage, I’ll grab it right there and eat it.’
sn20.10:2.3: Then a mouse came out to forage.
sn20.10:2.4: The cat, catching it right away, swallowed it without chewing it.
sn20.10:2.5: The mouse chewed on its small & large intestines,
sn20.10:2.6: and from that cause there came [for the cat] death & death-like pain.
sn20.10:3.1: “In the same way, there is the case where a monk, early in the morning, having adjusted his lower robe and, carrying his bowl & outer robe, enters a village or town for alms—with his bodily actions unprotected, his speech unprotected, his mind unprotected, with mindfulness unestablished, with his sense faculties unguarded.
sn20.10:3.2: There he sees a woman improperly dressed or half-naked.
sn20.10:3.3: As he sees her improperly dressed or half-naked, lust ravages his mind.
sn20.10:3.4: When his mind is ravaged by lust, death comes to him or death-like pain.
sn20.10:3.5: Monks, ‘death’ in this discipline of a noble one is when one renounces the training and returns to the lower life.
sn20.10:3.6: ‘Death-like pain’ is when one falls into a certain defiled offense
sn20.10:3.9: ‘We will enter a village or town for alms with our bodily actions protected, our speech protected, our mind protected, with mindfulness established, with our sense faculties guarded.’
sn20.10:3.10: That’s how you should train yourselves.”
